evals.name: morar-vs-viver-drill
evals.prompt: Create a 5-question fill-in-the-blank drill teaching when to use 'morar' vs 'viver' in Brazilian Portuguese, A1 level. Use the JSON format that matches the other drills in config/prompts/ (look at one or two for reference). Save the JSON to the path I give you.
evals.expected_output: A drill JSON with 5 fill-in-the-blank items that respect the native usage filter (morar = address/residence, viver = abstract life), each blank forced (only one verb fits), no fake accents, BP variant, English glosses for each PT word.

evals.name: open-closed-e-primer
evals.prompt: Write a primer section explaining the open É vs closed Ê distinction in Brazilian Portuguese for an A2 learner. Include phonetic respellings. Output as plain markdown.
evals.expected_output: A primer section with paulista-only respellings, real spellings only (no fake accents like 'êle'), one claim per teaching block, contrast examples that share phonological category, English translations for every PT word.

evals.name: ep-cafe-worksheet
evals.prompt: Draft a worksheet introduction for ordering food in a Lisbon café — A1 European Portuguese. Include a short dialogue (4-6 lines). Output as plain markdown.
evals.expected_output: An EP (not BP) worksheet intro: uses 'tu' and EP verb forms, EP vocabulary (bica/imperial/se faz favor), generic learner audience (no specific student name), every PT line glossed in English, no fake accents.
